id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0003
text: Innovations such as smart screens and retail media networks attract extra audiences and new applications of artificial intelligence also drive growth. The media offering is increasingly digital, in other words expressible in “zeros and ones”.

metadata.chunk_id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0003
metadata.source_slug: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen
metadata.source_title: Digital media grow through transformation and new channels
metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Digitalisering
metadata.author: Mario Bersem
metadata.description: Digital media are growing strongly through transformation and new channels, while traditional media are shrinking. Trends and outlook. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Innovations such as smart screens and retail media networks attract extra audiences and new applications of artificial intelligence also drive growth. The media offering is increasingly digital, in other words expressible in “zeros and ones”. On the one hand, this transformation means digital media grow at the expense of traditional media, for example when paper newspaper subscriptions are converted to digital newspaper subscriptions. On the other hand, digital media grow thanks to innovations that attract extra audiences. For 2024, ABN AMRO expects revenue growth of 9 per cent for digital media, while traditional media will shrink by 1 per cent. On balance, the media sector as a whole will post revenue growth of 6 per cent, supported by a strong recovery in consumer purchasing power. In 2025, revenue growth for digital media is expected to ease slightly to 7 per cent, partly due to lower inflation. [Also read: Media sector grows despite stagnating economy](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/technologie/mediasector-groeit-ondanks-stagnerende-economie.html).

id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0010
text: One of these innovations is smart “digital out-of-home screens” that show ads depending on the time of day or even how busy the street is. New retail media networks are also growing strongly; retail chains are making more and more ad space available in their own channels such as the website, the app, and also the screens in shops.

Search engines and social media platforms are also still growing in use, purely digital channels that have no clear analogue counterpart. Transformation of the media landscape. The media landscape is marked by a transformation that consists of a shift from analogue to digital, from offline to online and from live to on-demand. Where people once mainly watched live on a television screen, they now also watch “television” via an app on the smartphone, while travelling on the train, two days after a programme has been broadcast.

id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0031
text: Digital ad spend drives media sector. Developments in the advertising market are a good barometer for developments within the media sector.

metadata.chunk_id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0031
metadata.source_slug: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen
metadata.source_title: Digital media grow through transformation and new channels
metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Digitalisering
metadata.author: Mario Bersem
metadata.description: Digital media are growing strongly through transformation and new channels, while traditional media are shrinking. Trends and outlook. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Digital ad spend drives media sector. Developments in the advertising market are a good barometer for developments within the media sector. In 2023, around € 5.2 billion was spent on advertising in the Netherlands, of which 71 per cent was digital advertising, that is € 3.7 billion, according to the VIA/Deloitte Digital Advertising Spend report for 2023 (see chart). Revenue growth for digital advertising was 5 per cent compared with 2022. Compared with an average annual growth rate of 11 per cent in the ten years before, this was relatively low. In 2024, ABN AMRO expects 9 per cent revenue growth for digital advertising, more in line with the historical average. Growth rates of 11 per cent or more from the past are no longer to be expected because digitalisation has now progressed further and some submarkets are saturated. Paid search, for example, is growing somewhat more slowly, while online video, social media and digital audio are growing somewhat faster.

[Also read: Advertising a lucrative side income for retail chains](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/advertenties-lucratieve-bijverdienste-voor-winkelketens.html). Paid search via search engines is the largest category within digital advertising and grew by 3 per cent in 2023 to around € 1.7 billion.

Paid search via search engines is the largest category within digital advertising and grew by 3 per cent in 2023 to around € 1.7 billion. Search engines thus accounted for one-third of total ad spend, online and offline, in 2023. Display advertising, including fast-growing categories such as online video (7 per cent growth) and social media (11 per cent growth), followed closely at € 1.6 billion and is expected to overtake search engines for the first time next year.

id: Digitale media groeien door transformatie en nieuwe kanalen__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0043
text: Digital audio grew by 16 per cent in 2023 due to the increasing popularity of podcasts and because FM radio is slowly giving way to digital DAB+ radio. In radio, the digital transformation is slower than elsewhere because people in the Netherlands enjoy listening to the radio in the car and many car radios are not yet equipped to receive DAB+.

metadata.parent_text: Digital audio grew by 16 per cent in 2023 due to the increasing popularity of podcasts and because FM radio is slowly giving way to digital DAB+ radio. In radio, the digital transformation is slower than elsewhere because people in the Netherlands enjoy listening to the radio in the car and many car radios are not yet equipped to receive DAB+. Since December 2020, every new car has been fitted with DAB+, following a European directive, but people in the Netherlands drive the [oldest car fleet in Western Europe](https://www.knac.nl/nederlands-heeft-oudste-wagenpark-in-west-europa-114-jaar/), with an average age of 11.4 years.
